questions.question: Define inheritance and explain its significance in OOP.
keyPoints: Inheritance is a mechanism in OOP that allows a class to inherit properties and behaviors from another class
It promotes code reusability by allowing derived classes to use base class members
It establishes IS-A relationship between classes
It supports extensibility allowing new classes to extend existing functionality

questions.question: Explain the 'protected' access specifier in C++ with an example.
keyPoints: Protected members are accessible within the class and its derived classes
They are not accessible from objects outside the class
Provides middle ground between private (most restrictive) and public (least restrictive)
Example: protected int age; - accessible in derived classes but not from main()

questions.question: List and explain the different types of inheritance in C++.
keyPoints: Single: One base class, one derived class
Multiple: One derived class inherits from multiple base classes
Multilevel: Chain of inheritance (A→B→C)
Hierarchical: One base class, multiple derived classes
Hybrid: Combination of two or more inheritance types

questions.question: What is the diamond problem? How is it solved in C++?
keyPoints: Diamond problem occurs in hybrid/multiple inheritance when a class inherits from two classes that share a common base
Creates ambiguity about which path to inherit common members
Solved using virtual base class: class Derived : virtual public Base1, virtual public Base2
Virtual ensures only one copy of base class members exists

questions.question: Explain the order of constructor and destructor execution in derived classes.
keyPoints: Constructors: Base class constructor executes first, then member constructors, then derived class constructor
Destructors: Derived class destructor executes first, then member destructors, then base class destructor
This order ensures proper initialization and cleanup
Reverse order of construction is natural for destruction

questions.question: What is ambiguity in multiple inheritance? How can it be resolved?
keyPoints: Ambiguity arises when derived class inherits same-named members from multiple base classes
Cannot determine which base class member is being referred to
Resolved using scope resolution operator (::)
Example: obj.Base1::show(); or obj.Base2::show();

questions.question: Differentiate between public, private, and protected inheritance.
keyPoints: Public inheritance: public→public, protected→protected, private→private (most common)
Protected inheritance: public→protected, protected→private, private→inaccessible
Private inheritance: public→private, protected→private, private→inaccessible
Determines accessibility of base class members in derived class

questions.question: Write a C++ program demonstrating single inheritance with a base class 'Person' and derived class 'Student'. Include appropriate constructors, destructors, and display functions.
keyPoints: Define base class Person with name, age data members
Define derived class Student with rollno, marks
Use public inheritance
Include constructors with initializer lists
Include destructors with appropriate messages
Create objects and demonstrate functionality
Show protected member usage

questions.question: Write a C++ program to demonstrate multiple inheritance with classes 'Teacher' and 'Student' deriving from 'Person', and a class 'TeachingAssistant' deriving from both.
keyPoints: Create base class Person
Create derived classes Teacher and Student
Create TeachingAssistant using multiple inheritance
Handle constructor execution order
Demonstrate ambiguity resolution if needed
Show proper use of virtual base class to avoid duplication

questions.question: Explain the concept of virtual base class with a suitable diagram and program to illustrate the diamond problem solution.
keyPoints: Explain diamond problem with class diagram
Show virtual keyword usage
Program with four classes: A (base), B and C (derived from A), D (derived from B and C)
Use virtual inheritance in B and C
Demonstrate single copy of A's members in D
Show constructor order with virtual base class

questions.question: Create a C++ program showing hierarchical inheritance with a base class 'Shape' and derived classes 'Circle', 'Rectangle', and 'Triangle'. Include virtual function for area calculation.
keyPoints: Define abstract base class Shape with virtual function area()
Derive Circle, Rectangle, Triangle from Shape
Use function overriding with virtual keyword
Demonstrate runtime polymorphism using base class pointer
Show how different derived class functions are called at runtime
